        pass "session-start failed gracefully with invalid JSON (exit code: $exit_code)"
    else
        fail "session-start should have failed with invalid JSON but succeeded"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 9: Error handling - missing session_id
test_missing_session_id() {
    log_test "Error handling - missing session_id"

    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start without session_id (should fail gracefully)
    local output
    local exit_code=0
    output=$(echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        pass "session-start failed gracefully with missing session_id (exit code: $exit_code)"
    else
        fail "session-start should have failed with missing session_id but succeeded"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 10: Performance - execution time under 100ms (target)
test_performance() {
    log_test "Performance - execution time"

    local session_id="test-session-010"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Measure execution time
    local start_time=$(date +%s%N)
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    local end_time=$(date +%s%N)

    local duration_ms=$(( (end_time - start_time) / 1000000 ))

    echo "  Execution time: ${duration_ms}ms"

    # Target is <100ms, but we'll be lenient during development
    # Resume feature with LLM call may take longer
    if [ $duration_ms -lt 5000 ]; then
        pass "Execution time acceptable: ${duration_ms}ms"
    else
        fail "Execution time too slow: ${duration_ms}ms (may include LLM call for resume)"
        # Don't return 1 - this is informational during development
    fi
}

# Main test runner
main() {
    echo "=================================="
    echo "Sidekick Session-Start Integration Test"
    echo "=================================="
    echo ""

    # Setup
    setup

    # Trap cleanup on exit
    trap cleanup EXIT

    # Run tests
    echo ""
    echo "Running tests..."
    echo ""

    test_session_start_basic || true
    test_session_directory_created || true
    test_tracking_initialized || true
    test_log_file_created || true
    test_feature_toggles || true
    test_resume_feature || true
    test_cleanup_launched || true
    test_invalid_json || true
    test_missing_session_id || true
    test_performance || true

    # Summary
    echo ""
    echo "=================================="
    echo "Test Summary"
    echo "=================================="
    echo "Tests run:    $TESTS_RUN"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}$TESTS_PASSED${NC}"
    echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}$TESTS_FAILED${NC}"
    echo "=================================="

    # Exit with failure if any tests failed
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo ""
        echo -e "${RED}Some tests failed. This is expected for TDD - implement the handler to fix them.${NC}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo ""
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${NC}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

# Run main
main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
# Integration test for session-start handler
# Tests the complete session-start workflow including all features

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Track test results
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Get script directory
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"

# Test helper functions
log_test() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}TEST:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))
}

pass() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ PASS:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
}

fail() {
    echo -e "${RED}✗ FAIL:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
}

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    log_test "Setting up test environment"

    # Create temporary test directory
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d -t sidekick-test-XXXXXX)
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$TEST_DIR"

    # Create sidekick structure in test directory
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/lib"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/handlers"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/prompts"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp"

    # Copy sidekick files to test directory
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/sidekick.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/lib/"
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/config.defaults" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"

    # Copy handlers if they exist
    if [ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers/session-start.sh" ]; then
        cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers/session-start.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/handlers/"
    fi

    # Copy features if they exist
    if [ -d "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features" ]; then
        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/"*.sh "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/" 2>/dev/null || true
        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/prompts/"* "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/prompts/" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    # Make sidekick.sh executable
    chmod +x "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh"

    # Create mock Claude CLI
    MOCK_CLAUDE="$TEST_DIR/mock-claude"
    cat > "$MOCK_CLAUDE" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Mock Claude CLI for testing
# Returns minimal valid JSON output

# Check if we're being called for resume snarkification
# (will have prompt containing "previous session")
if grep -q "previous session" <<< "$@" 2>/dev/null; then
    cat <<'JSON'
```json
{
  "session_id": "test-resume-123",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T12:00:00Z",
  "initial_goal": "Test previous session goal",
  "current_objective": "Resume testing",
  "clarity_score": 8,
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "snarky_comment": "Back for more punishment, eh?"
}
```
JSON
else
    # Default topic extraction response
    cat <<'JSON'
```json
{
  "session_id": "test-123",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T12:00:00Z",
  "task_ids": ["TEST-001"],
  "initial_goal": "Test goal",
  "current_objective": "Testing session start",
  "clarity_score": 7,
  "confidence": 0.85
}
```
JSON
fi
EOF
    chmod +x "$MOCK_CLAUDE"
    export CLAUDE_BIN="$MOCK_CLAUDE"

    # Create test config with all features enabled
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
# Test configuration with all features enabled
FEATURE_TRACKING=true
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true
FEATURE_RESUME=true
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Disable sleeper for testing (we don't want background processes during test)
SLEEPER_ENABLED=false

# Set log level to debug for testing
LOG_LEVEL=debug

# Override cleanup to dry-run for testing
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=true
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=1
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=0
EOF

    pass "Test environment created at $TEST_DIR"
}

# Cleanup test environment
cleanup() {
    log_test "Cleaning up test environment"

    # Kill any background processes we may have started
    if [ -n "${TEST_DIR:-}" ] && [ -d "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp" ]; then
        find "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp" -name "*.pid" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r pidfile; do
            if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then
                pid=$(cat "$pidfile" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
                if [ -n "$pid" ] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
                    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
                fi
            fi
        done
    fi

    # Remove test directory
    if [ -n "${TEST_DIR:-}" ] && [ -d "$TEST_DIR" ]; then
        rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
        pass "Test environment cleaned up"
    fi
}

# Test 1: Basic session-start execution
test_session_start_basic() {
    log_test "Basic session-start execution"

    local session_id="test-session-001"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    local output
    if output=$(echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start 2>&1); then
        pass "session-start executed without errors"
    else
        fail "session-start failed with exit code $?: $output"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 2: Session directory creation
test_session_directory_created() {
    log_test "Session directory creation"

    local session_id="test-session-002"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if session directory was created
    local session_dir="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id"
    if [ -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        pass "Session directory created: $session_dir"
    else
        fail "Session directory not created: $session_dir"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 3: Tracking counter initialization (if enabled)
test_tracking_initialized() {
    log_test "Tracking counter initialization"

    local session_id="test-session-003"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if counter file was created
    local counter_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"
    if [ -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        local count=$(cat "$counter_file")
        if [ "$count" = "0" ]; then
            pass "Tracking counter initialized to 0"
        else
            fail "Tracking counter has wrong value: $count (expected 0)"
            return 1
        fi
    else
        fail "Tracking counter file not created: $counter_file"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 4: Log file creation
test_log_file_created() {
    log_test "Log file creation"

    local session_id="test-session-004"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if log file was created
    local log_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/sidekick.log"
    if [ -f "$log_file" ]; then
        pass "Log file created: $log_file"

        # Check log file has content
        if [ -s "$log_file" ]; then
            pass "Log file has content"
        else
            fail "Log file is empty"
            return 1
        fi
    else
        fail "Log file not created: $log_file"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 5: Feature toggles respected
test_feature_toggles() {
    log_test "Feature toggles respected"

    # Create config with tracking disabled
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
FEATURE_TRACKING=false
FEATURE_CLEANUP=false
FEATURE_RESUME=false
LOG_LEVEL=debug
EOF

    local session_id="test-session-005"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check that counter file was NOT created (tracking disabled)
    local counter_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/response_count"
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        pass "Tracking disabled - counter file not created"
    else
        fail "Tracking disabled but counter file was created"
        return 1
    fi

    # Re-enable features for remaining tests
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
FEATURE_TRACKING=true
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true
FEATURE_RESUME=true
LOG_LEVEL=debug
SLEEPER_ENABLED=false
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=true
EOF
}

# Test 6: Resume feature (if enabled and previous session exists)
test_resume_feature() {
    log_test "Resume feature"

    local prev_session_id="test-session-006a"
    local new_session_id="test-session-006b"

    # Create a previous session with a topic file
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$prev_session_id"
    cat > "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$prev_session_id/topic.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "session_id": "test-session-006a",
  "timestamp": "2025-10-22T10:00:00Z",
  "initial_goal": "Previous session goal",
  "current_objective": "Testing resume",
  "clarity_score": 8,
  "confidence": 0.9
}
EOF

    # Start new session
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON
{
  "session_id": "$new_session_id",
  "workspace": {
    "project_dir": "$TEST_DIR",
    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if new session has topic file (created by resume feature)
    local topic_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$new_session_id/topic.json"
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        pass "Resume created topic file for new session"

        # Verify it contains resume information
        if grep -q "snarky_comment" "$topic_file"; then
            pass "Topic file contains snarky resume comment"
        else
            # This might be okay - snarky_comment is optional
            pass "Topic file created (snarky comment optional)"
        fi
    else
        # This might fail if resume feature is not implemented yet
        fail "Resume topic file not created: $topic_file (may not be implemented yet)"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test 7: Cleanup launched in background (if enabled)
test_cleanup_launched() {
    log_test "Cleanup launched in background"

    local session_id="test-session-007"
    local test_json=$(cat <<JSON

  }

JSON
)

    # Execute session-start
    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Check if cleanup PID file was created
    local cleanup_pid_file="$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/$session_id/cleanup.pid"
    if [ -f "$cleanup_pid_file" ]; then
        pass "Cleanup PID file created: $cleanup_pid_file"

        # Check if process is running
        local pid=$(cat "$cleanup_pid_file")
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then

  }

    output=$(echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then

test_performance() {

{

    "current_dir": "$TEST_DIR"
  }
}
JSON
)

    # Measure execution time

    echo "$test_json" | "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh" session-start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    else

        # Don't return 1 - this is informational during development
    fi
}

# Main test runner
main() {
    echo "=================================="

    setup

    echo "Running tests..."

    test_cleanup_launched || true

#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Tracking
# Request counting and static reminder injection

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_LIB_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before tracking.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_init - Initialize tracking counter for a session
#
# Creates the response_count file and initializes it to 0
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_init() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_init: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Initialize counter to 0
    echo "0" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking initialized: $counter_file"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_increment - Increment counter and return new value
#
# Atomically increments the counter and returns the new value
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the new counter value to stdout
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_increment() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_increment: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        log_warn "Counter file not found, initializing: $counter_file"
        tracking_init "$session_id" || return 1
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    # Increment
    local new_value=$((current + 1))

    # Write back
    echo "$new_value" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking incremented: $current -> $new_value"

    # Output new value
    echo "$new_value"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_get - Get current counter value
#
# Reads and returns the current counter value without incrementing
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the current counter value to stdout (0 if not initialized)
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_get() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_get: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$current"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_check_reminder - Check if static reminder is due
#
# Checks if we should output a static reminder based on the counter value
# and configured cadence. Loads static reminder text from files.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - count (current response count)
#   $2 - project_dir (optional, for project-specific reminders)
#
# Returns:
#   Prints reminder text to stdout if due, empty if not
#   Exit code: 0 always
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_check_reminder() {
    local count="$1"
    local project_dir="${2:-}"

    # Get cadence from config (default to 4)
    local cadence
    cadence=$(config_get "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE")
    [ -z "$cadence" ] && cadence=4

    # Check if reminder is due (count is multiple of cadence, but not 0)
    if [ "$count" -gt 0 ] && [ $((count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_debug "Static reminder due at count $count (cadence $cadence)"

        # Try to load reminder text from files (project overrides user)
        local reminder_text=""

        # Try project-specific reminder first
        if [ -n "$project_dir" ]; then
            local project_reminder="$project_dir/.claude/hooks/sidekick/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$project_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$project_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded project reminder from $project_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Fall back to user reminder if no project reminder
        if [ -z "$reminder_text" ]; then
            local sidekick_root
            sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
            local user_reminder="$sidekick_root/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$user_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$user_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded user reminder from $user_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Output reminder if we have one
        if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
            echo "$reminder_text"
        else
            log_debug "No static reminder file found"
        fi
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Tracking feature loaded"

#!/bin/bash
# Cleanup old session directories from tmp/
# This script performs garbage collection on session directories, removing old sessions
# when the count of old sessions exceeds a threshold. Age is measured by the newest file
# in each session directory.
#
# Usage: cleanup-old-sessions.sh <output_base_dir> [--verbose]
#   output_base_dir: Parent directory of tmp/ (e.g., /project/.claude/hooks/reminders)
#   --verbose, -v: Enable detailed logging
#
# Configuration via environment variables:
#   CLAUDE_TMP_CLEANUP_ENABLED (default: true) - Enable/disable cleanup
#   CLAUDE_TMP_CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT (default: 5) - Minimum old sessions before cleanup triggers
#   CLAUDE_TMP_CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS (default: 2) - Age threshold in days
#   CLAUDE_TMP_CLEANUP_DRY_RUN (default: false) - Test mode, don't actually delete
#   VERBOSE (default: false) - Enable detailed logging
#
# This script is designed to run as a detached background process (via nohup) from
# response-tracker.sh during session initialization.

# Strict error handling (but allow grep to fail gracefully)
set -euo pipefail

# Parse command line arguments
VERBOSE=${VERBOSE:-false}

# First argument is required (output_base_dir)
if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "[CleanupSessions] ERROR: Missing required argument: output_base_dir" >&2
    exit 1
fi

output_base_dir="$1"
shift  # Remove output_base_dir from args

# Parse remaining flags
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --verbose|-v)
            VERBOSE=true
            shift
            ;;
        *)
            echo "[CleanupSessions] WARNING: Unknown option: $1" >&2
            shift
            ;;
    esac
done

# Export VERBOSE so child processes inherit it
export VERBOSE

tmp_dir="${output_base_dir}/tmp"

# Configuration
CLEANUP_ENABLED=${CLAUDE_TMP_CLEANUP_ENABLED:-true}
MIN_COUNT=${CLAUDE_TMP_CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT:-5}
AGE_DAYS=${CLAUDE_TMP_CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS:-2}
DRY_RUN=${CLAUDE_TMP_CLEANUP_DRY_RUN:-false}
SKIP_SAFETY=${CLAUDE_TMP_CLEANUP_SKIP_SAFETY:-false}  # For testing only

# Logging helpers
# NOTE: log_debug must return 0 even when VERBOSE=false to avoid triggering set -e
log_debug() {

    age_days=$((age / 86400))
    session_id=$(basename "$session_dir")

    if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
        log_info "[DRY-RUN] Would remove: $session_id (${age_days} days old)"
    else
        log_info "Removing: $session_id (${age_days} days old)"
        if rm -rf "$session_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
            removed_count=$((removed_count + 1))
        else
            log_error "Failed to remove: $session_dir"
        fi
    fi
done

if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
    log_info "Dry-run complete: would have removed $sessions_to_remove sessions"
else
    log_info "Cleanup complete: removed $removed_count of $sessions_to_remove sessions"
fi

exit 0

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Cleanup
# Session directory garbage collection

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_CLEANUP_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_CLEANUP_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_LIB_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before cleanup.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# cleanup_launch - Launch cleanup process in background
#
# Launches the cleanup_run function as a background process with PID tracking
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cleanup_launch() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "cleanup_launch: session_id and project_dir are required"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if cleanup is enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "cleanup"; then
        log_debug "Cleanup feature is disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    local cleanup_enabled
    cleanup_enabled=$(config_get "CLEANUP_ENABLED")
    if [ "$cleanup_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleanup is disabled (CLEANUP_ENABLED=$cleanup_enabled)"
        return 0
    fi

    log_info "Launching cleanup process in background"

    # Launch cleanup_run as background process
    # Export necessary vars for subprocess
    export SIDEKICK_ROOT
    export LOG_LEVEL
    SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Launch the cleanup process
    # We need to source this file in the subprocess so it has the cleanup_run function
    process_launch_background "$session_id" "cleanup" bash -c "
        # Source the common library and this feature in the subprocess
        source '$SIDEKICK_ROOT/lib/common.sh'
        source '$SIDEKICK_ROOT/features/cleanup.sh'

        # Re-load config
        config_load

        # Re-initialize logging for subprocess
        log_init '$session_id'

        # Run cleanup
        cleanup_run '$project_dir'
    "

    log_debug "Cleanup process launched with PID file"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# cleanup_run - Main cleanup logic
#
# Performs garbage collection on old session directories
# This function is called in the background process
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - project_dir (to locate sidekick tmp directory)
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cleanup_run() {
    local project_dir="$1"

    if [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "cleanup_run: project_dir is required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "Starting cleanup process"

    # Get configuration
    local min_count age_days dry_run
    min_count=$(config_get "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT")
    age_days=$(config_get "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS")
    dry_run=$(config_get "CLEANUP_DRY_RUN")

    # Defaults
    [ -z "$min_count" ] && min_count=5
    [ -z "$age_days" ] && age_days=2
    [ -z "$dry_run" ] && dry_run=false

    log_debug "Cleanup config: min_count=$min_count, age_days=$age_days, dry_run=$dry_run"

    # Determine tmp directory path
    # Could be user scope or project scope
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local tmp_dir="$sidekick_root/tmp"

    log_debug "Scanning tmp directory: $tmp_dir"

    # Verify tmp directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$tmp_dir" ]; then
        log_debug "No tmp directory found at: $tmp_dir"
        return 0
    fi

    # Safety check - must be under sidekick directory
    if [[ ! "$tmp_dir" =~ /.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp$ ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid tmp_dir path (safety check failed): $tmp_dir"
        return 1
    fi

    # Find all session directories matching UUID pattern
    # UUID format: 8-4-4-4-12 hex digits
    local uuid_pattern="[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}"
    local session_dirs=()

    while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do
        local basename
        basename=$(basename "$dir")
        # Validate UUID pattern for safety
        if [[ "$basename" =~ ^$uuid_pattern$ ]]; then
            session_dirs+=("$dir")
        else
            log_warn "Skipping non-UUID directory: $basename"
        fi
    done < <(find "$tmp_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -print0 2>/dev/null || true)

    local total_count=${#session_dirs[@]}
    log_debug "Found $total_count session directories"

    if [ $total_count -eq 0 ]; then
        log_debug "No session directories to process"
        return 0
    fi

    # Calculate age for each session directory
    # Age is based on the newest file in the directory
    local current_time
    current_time=$(date +%s)
    local age_threshold_seconds=$((age_days * 24 * 60 * 60))

    declare -A session_ages  # session_dir -> age_in_seconds
    local old_sessions=()

    for session_dir in "${session_dirs[@]}"; do
        # Find newest file in session directory
        # Using -printf '%T@' to get modification time as Unix timestamp
        local newest_file_time
        newest_file_time=$(find "$session_dir" -type f -printf '%T@\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -nr | head -1)

        if [ -z "$newest_file_time" ]; then
            # Empty directory or no files - use directory mtime
            newest_file_time=$(stat -c '%Y' "$session_dir" 2>/dev/null || echo "$current_time")
        fi

        # Convert to integer (truncate fractional seconds)
        newest_file_time=${newest_file_time%.*}

        # Calculate age in seconds
        local age=$((current_time - newest_file_time))
        session_ages["$session_dir"]=$age

        # Filter sessions older than threshold
        if [ $age -ge $age_threshold_seconds ]; then
            old_sessions+=("$session_dir")
            local age_days_actual=$((age / 86400))
            log_debug "Old session: $(basename "$session_dir") (${age_days_actual} days old)"
        fi
    done

    local old_count=${#old_sessions[@]}
    log_info "Found $old_count old sessions (>$age_days days) out of $total_count total"

    # Only cleanup if we exceed the minimum count threshold
    if [ $old_count -le $min_count ]; then
        log_info "Old session count ($old_count) <= threshold ($min_count), no cleanup needed"
        return 0
    fi

    # Sort old sessions by age (oldest first) for deletion
    # We'll remove sessions to bring the old count down to the threshold
    local sessions_to_remove=$((old_count - min_count))
    log_info "Will remove $sessions_to_remove oldest sessions to reach threshold of $min_count"

    # Create array of [age session_dir] for sorting
    local age_dir_pairs=()
    for session_dir in "${old_sessions[@]}"; do
        local age=${session_ages["$session_dir"]}
        age_dir_pairs+=("$age $session_dir")
    done

    # Sort by age (descending) and extract session directories
    IFS=$'\n' local sorted_pairs=($(sort -rn <<<"${age_dir_pairs[*]}"))
    unset IFS

    # Remove the oldest sessions
    local removed_count=0
    for i in $(seq 0 $((sessions_to_remove - 1))); do
        local pair="${sorted_pairs[$i]}"
        local session_dir="${pair#* }"  # Extract session_dir from "age session_dir"
        local age=${session_ages["$session_dir"]}
        local age_days_actual=$((age / 86400))
        local session_id
        session_id=$(basename "$session_dir")

        if [ "$dry_run" = "true" ]; then
            log_info "[DRY-RUN] Would remove: $session_id (${age_days_actual} days old)"
        else
            log_info "Removing: $session_id (${age_days_actual} days old)"
            if rm -rf "$session_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
                removed_count=$((removed_count + 1))
            else
                log_error "Failed to remove: $session_dir"
            fi
        fi
    done

    if [ "$dry_run" = "true" ]; then
        log_info "Dry-run complete: would have removed $sessions_to_remove sessions"
    else
        log_info "Cleanup complete: removed $removed_count of $sessions_to_remove sessions"
    fi

    return 0
}

            log_info "Removing: $session_id (${age_days_actual} days old)"

                removed_count=$((removed_count + 1))
            else
                log_error "Failed to remove: $session_dir"
            fi
        fi
    done

    if [ "$dry_run" = "true" ]; then
        log_info "Dry-run complete: would have removed $sessions_to_remove sessions"
    else
        log_info "Cleanup complete: removed $removed_count of $sessions_to_remove sessions"
    fi

    return 0
}

#!/bin/bash
# Snarkify last session - creates resume statusline from previous session
# Runs on SessionStart to proactively generate resume message with personality
# Finds last topic file with good clarity and generates snarky resume comment

set -euo pipefail

# Script metadata
readonly SCRIPT_VERSION="1.0.0"
readonly SCRIPT_NAME="$(basename "$0")"

# Parse command-line arguments
usage() {
    cat <<EOF
Usage: $SCRIPT_NAME [project_dir]

Arguments:
  project_dir  (Optional) Project root directory for .claude/hooks/reminders/tmp
               Falls back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable if not provided

Reads JSON from stdin with format: {"session_id":"..."}

Example:
  echo '{"session_id":"abc-123"}' | $SCRIPT_NAME /path/to/project
  echo '{"session_id":"abc-123"}' | CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=/path/to/project $SCRIPT_NAME
EOF
    exit 1
}

# Handle --help flag
if [ "${1:-}" = "--help" ] || [ "${1:-}" = "-h" ]; then
    usage
fi

# Configuration
VERBOSE="${VERBOSE:-false}"
DRY_RUN="${CLAUDE_SNARK_DRY_RUN:-false}"
SNARK_MODEL="${CLAUDE_SNARK_MODEL:-haiku}"

# Paths
readonly SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

# Resolve Claude binary path (aliases not available in non-interactive shells)
CLAUDE_BIN="${CLAUDE_BIN:-$HOME/.claude/local/claude}"
if [ ! -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
    # Fallback: try to find in common locations
    for path in "$HOME/.claude/local/claude" "$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null)"; do
        if [ -x "$path" ]; then
            CLAUDE_BIN="$path"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

# ANSI color codes
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'

# Get project directory from parameter (preferred) or environment variable (fallback)
project_dir="${1:-${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}}"

if [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [WARN] No project directory provided (parameter or CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR), cannot locate cache directory" >&2
    exit 0
fi

# Read session_id from stdin JSON (SessionStart hook input) BEFORE any logging
input=$(cat)
session_id=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.session_id' 2>/dev/null || echo "")

# Create session-specific directory for logs
session_dir="${project_dir}/.claude/hooks/reminders/tmp/${session_id}"
mkdir -p "$session_dir" 2>/dev/null || true

# Set log file to session-specific directory
LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/snarkify.log"

# Logging functions (defined AFTER LOG_FILE is set)
log() {
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [INFO] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"
    if [ "$VERBOSE" = true ]; then
        echo -e "${COLOR_GREEN}[Snarkify]${COLOR_RESET} $*" >&2
    fi
    return 0
}

log_warn() {
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [WARN] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"
    if [ "$VERBOSE" = true ]; then
        echo -e "${COLOR_YELLOW}[Snarkify] WARN:${COLOR_RESET} $*" >&2
    fi
    return 0
}

log_debug() {
    if [ "$VERBOSE" = true ]; then
        echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [DEBUG] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo -e "${COLOR_GRAY}[Snarkify] DEBUG:${COLOR_RESET} $*" >&2

echo -e "${COLOR_GRAY}[Snarkify] DEBUG:${COLOR_RESET} $*" >&2
    fi
    return 0
}

if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
    log_warn "No session_id found in input, exiting"
    exit 0
fi

log "Starting snarkify for session: $session_id"

# Determine cache directory and current session directory
cache_dir="${project_dir}/.claude/hooks/reminders/tmp"
current_session_dir="${cache_dir}/${session_id}"
current_topic="${current_session_dir}/topic.json"

log_debug "Cache directory: $cache_dir"
log_debug "Current session directory: $current_session_dir"
log_debug "Current topic file: $current_topic"

# Skip if current topic already exists
if [ -f "$current_topic" ]; then
    log_debug "Current session topic already exists, skipping"
    exit 0
fi

# Ensure cache directory exists
mkdir -p "$cache_dir" || {
    log_warn "Failed to create cache directory: $cache_dir"
    exit 1
}

# Find most recent topic file with clarity > 5 from OTHER sessions
# Search all session directories except current one
last_topic=$(find "$cache_dir" -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -name "topic.json" -type f 2>/dev/null | \
    grep -v "/${session_id}/" | \
    while read -r file; do
        clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
        timestamp=$(jq -r '.timestamp // ""' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        if [ "$clarity" -gt 5 ] && [ -n "$timestamp" ]; then
            echo "${timestamp}|${file}"
        fi
    done | sort -r | head -1 | cut -d'|' -f2)

if [ -z "$last_topic" ] || [ ! -f "$last_topic" ]; then
    log_debug "No previous session found with clarity > 5"
    exit 0
fi

log "Found previous session topic: $last_topic"

# Load prompt template
prompt_template="${SCRIPT_DIR}/analysis-prompts/new-session-topic.txt"
if [ ! -f "$prompt_template" ]; then
    log_warn "Prompt template not found: $prompt_template, using fallback"
    # Ensure session directory exists
    mkdir -p "$current_session_dir" || {
        log_warn "Failed to create session directory: $current_session_dir"
        exit 1
    }
    # Fallback to manual construction
    last_goal=$(jq -r '.current_objective // .initial_goal // "previous work"' "$last_topic" 2>/dev/null || echo "previous work")
    cat > "$current_topic" <<EOF
{
  "session_id": "${session_id}",
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")",
  "task_ids": [],
  "initial_goal": "Shall we resume ${last_goal}?",
  "current_objective": "Navigate the space-time continuum of code",
  "intent_category": "development",
  "clarity_score": 6,
  "confidence": 0.7,
  "high_clarity_snarky_comment": null,
  "low_clarity_snarky_comment": "Back to work, because apparently we weren't done the first time",
  "resume_from_session": true
}
EOF
    log "Created resume topic file (fallback): $current_topic"
    exit 0
fi

# Build analysis prompt by combining template and previous topic JSON
# Use here-doc to properly handle multi-line JSON
analysis_prompt=$(cat "$prompt_template")
analysis_prompt="${analysis_prompt/\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$(cat "$last_topic")}"

log_debug "Generating resume topic with model: $SNARK_MODEL (dry_run=$DRY_RUN)"

# Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
create_isolated_workspace() {
    local workspace_dir="$1"

    mkdir -p "$workspace_dir/.claude" || {
        log_warn "Failed to create workspace directory: $workspace_dir"
        return 1
    }

    # Disable hooks and statusline to prevent recursion
    cat > "$workspace_dir/.claude/settings.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusLine": null
}
EOF
    return 0
}

workspace_dir="/tmp/claude-snarkify-$$-${session_id}"
create_isolated_workspace "$workspace_dir" || {
    log_warn "Failed to create isolated workspace, using dry-run fallback"
    DRY_RUN="true"
}

# Call Claude for analysis (or use dry-run fallback)
if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "true" ]; then
    # Dry-run fallback
    last_goal=$(jq -r '.current_objective // .initial_goal // "previous work"' "$last_topic" 2>/dev/null || echo "previous work")
    initial_goal="Shall we resume ${last_goal}?"
    current_objective="Navigate the space-time continuum of code"
    snark="[DRY-RUN] Back to ${last_goal}, because apparently we weren't done the first time"
    log "Using dry-run values"
else
    # Call LLM with prompt in isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    llm_output=$(cd "$workspace_dir" && echo "$analysis_prompt" | "$CLAUDE_BIN" -p --model "$SNARK_MODEL" --setting-sources project 2>>"$LOG_FILE")

    log_debug "Raw LLM output: $llm_output"

    # Extract JSON (try markdown code block first, then raw JSON)
    llm_json=$(echo "$llm_output" | sed -n '/```json/,/```/p' | sed '1d;$d')
    if [ -z "$llm_json" ]; then
        # Fallback: try extracting raw JSON
        llm_json=$(echo "$llm_output" | sed -n '/^{/,/^}/p')
    fi

    log_debug "Extracted JSON: $llm_json"

    if [ -z "$llm_json" ] || ! echo "$llm_json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "LLM returned no valid JSON, using fallback"
        last_goal=$(jq -r '.current_objective // .initial_goal // "previous work"' "$last_topic" 2>/dev/null || echo "previous work")
        initial_goal="Shall we resume ${last_goal}?"
        current_objective="Navigate the space-time continuum of code"
        snark="Ah yes, picking up where we left off. What could possibly go wrong?"
    else
        # Extract fields from LLM response
        initial_goal=$(echo "$llm_json" | jq -r '.initial_goal // ""')
        current_objective=$(echo "$llm_json" | jq -r '.current_objective // ""')
        snark=$(echo "$llm_json" | jq -r '.snarky_comment // ""')

        # Validate we got values

     4→1. A concise resume message for the statusline

    10→Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no explanation) with this exact schema:
    11→

    14→  "current_objective": "string (cleverly creative new SciFi-themed objective related to the goal (max 60 chars))",
    15→  "snarky_comment": "string (witty, sarcastic comment about your relationship to the user, max 120 chars)"
    16→}
    17→
    18→Requirements:
    19→- Extract the most meaningful goal/objective from previous session (prefer current_objective over initial_goal if more specific)
    20→- initial_goal: Must be in the form of a question, e.g. "Shall we resume" or "Want to continue" followed by brief description (max 60 chars total)
    21→- current_objective: Related to the initial_goal, but an imaginary, creative, SciFi-themed objective (max 60 chars total)
    22→- snarky_comment: Channel sarcastic software architect with SciFi references, acknowledging the odd relationship you and the user have. Be creative.

You are creating a resume topic for a new Claude Code session based on a previous session's goal.

Your task is to analyze the previous session's topic data and generate:
1. A concise resume message for the statusline
2. A witty snarky comment about resuming this work

PREVIOUS SESSION TOPIC:
{PREVIOUS_TOPIC}

Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no explanation) with this exact schema:

{
  "initial_goal": "string ("shall we resume...?" message referencing previous work, max 60 chars)",
  "current_objective": "string (cleverly creative new SciFi-themed objective related to the goal (max 60 chars))",
  "snarky_comment": "string (witty, sarcastic comment about your relationship to the user, max 120 chars)"
}

Requirements:
- Extract the most meaningful goal/objective from previous session (prefer current_objective over initial_goal if more specific)
- initial_goal: Must be in the form of a question, e.g. "Shall we resume" or "Want to continue" followed by brief description (max 60 chars total)
- current_objective: Related to the initial_goal, but an imaginary, creative, SciFi-themed objective (max 60 chars total)
- snarky_comment: Channel sarcastic software architect with SciFi references, acknowledging the odd relationship you and the user have. Be creative.

#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Resume
# Session continuity and snarkification

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_RESUME_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_RESUME_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_LIB_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before resume.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# resume_snarkify - Generate resume topic from previous session
#
# Finds the most recent previous session with good clarity and generates
# a snarky resume message for the new session
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id (current session)
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success or skip, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
resume_snarkify() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "resume_snarkify: session_id and project_dir are required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "Starting resume snarkify for session: $session_id"

    # Get session directory
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local current_topic="$session_dir/topic.json"

    # Skip if current topic already exists
    if [ -f "$current_topic" ]; then
        log_debug "Current session topic already exists, skipping resume"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get sidekick root to find tmp directory
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local tmp_dir="$sidekick_root/tmp"

    log_debug "Searching for previous session in: $tmp_dir"

    # Ensure tmp directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$tmp_dir" ]; then
        log_debug "No tmp directory found, cannot resume"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local min_clarity resume_model
    min_clarity=$(config_get "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY")
    resume_model=$(config_get "RESUME_MODEL")

    # Defaults
    [ -z "$min_clarity" ] && min_clarity=5
    [ -z "$resume_model" ] && resume_model="haiku-4.5"

    log_debug "Resume config: min_clarity=$min_clarity, model=$resume_model"

    # Find most recent topic file with clarity > threshold from OTHER sessions
    # Search all session directories except current one
    local last_topic
    last_topic=$(find "$tmp_dir" -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -name "topic.json" -type f 2>/dev/null | \
        grep -v "/${session_id}/" | \
        while read -r file; do
            local clarity timestamp
            clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            timestamp=$(jq -r '.timestamp // ""' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
            if [ "$clarity" -gt "$min_clarity" ] && [ -n "$timestamp" ]; then
                echo "${timestamp}|${file}"
            fi
        done | sort -r | head -1 | cut -d'|' -f2)

    if [ -z "$last_topic" ] || [ ! -f "$last_topic" ]; then
        log_debug "No previous session found with clarity > $min_clarity"
        return 0
    fi

    log_info "Found previous session topic: $last_topic"

    # Load prompt template
    local prompt_template="$sidekick_root/features/prompts/new-session-topic.txt"
    if [ ! -f "$prompt_template" ]; then
        log_warn "Prompt template not found: $prompt_template, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    # Read previous topic content
    local prev_topic_content
    if ! prev_topic_content=$(cat "$last_topic" 2>/dev/null); then
        log_error "Failed to read previous topic file: $last_topic"
        return 1
    fi

    # Build analysis prompt by substituting previous topic
    local analysis_prompt
    analysis_prompt=$(cat "$prompt_template")
    analysis_prompt="${analysis_prompt/\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$prev_topic_content}"

    log_debug "Calling LLM for resume snarkification (model: $resume_model)"

    # Call Claude for analysis
    local llm_output
    if ! llm_output=$(claude_invoke "$resume_model" "$analysis_prompt" 30); then
        log_warn "LLM invocation failed, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "LLM output received, extracting JSON"

    # Extract JSON from output
    local llm_json
    if ! llm_json=$(claude_extract_json "$llm_output"); then
        log_warn "Failed to extract JSON from LLM output, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON
    if ! echo "$llm_json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "Invalid JSON from LLM, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    # Extract fields from LLM response
    local initial_goal current_objective snark
    initial_goal=$(echo "$llm_json" | jq -r '.initial_goal // ""')
    current_objective=$(echo "$llm_json" | jq -r '.current_objective // ""')
    snark=$(echo "$llm_json" | jq -r '.snarky_comment // ""')

    # Validate we got values
    if [ -z "$initial_goal" ] || [ -z "$current_objective" ]; then
        log_warn "Missing required fields from LLM response, using fallback"
        _resume_fallback "$session_id" "$last_topic" "$current_topic"
        return 0
    fi

    # Create resume topic JSON
    cat > "$current_topic" <<EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")",
  "task_ids": [],
  "initial_goal": "$initial_goal",
  "current_objective": "$current_objective",
  "intent_category": "development",
  "clarity_score": 7,
  "confidence": 0.8,
  "snarky_comment": "$snark",
  "resume_from_session": true
}
EOF

    log_info "Created resume topic file: $current_topic"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# _resume_fallback - Create fallback resume topic
#
# Internal function to create a simple resume topic when LLM fails
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - last_topic_file (path to previous topic)
#   $3 - current_topic_file (path to write to)
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
_resume_fallback() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local last_topic="$2"
    local current_topic="$3"

    log_debug "Using fallback resume topic creation"

    # Extract previous goal
    local last_goal
    last_goal=$(jq -r '.current_objective // .initial_goal // "previous work"' "$last_topic" 2>/dev/null || echo "previous work")

    # Create fallback topic
    cat > "$current_topic" <<EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")",
  "task_ids": [],
  "initial_goal": "Shall we resume ${last_goal}?",
  "current_objective": "Navigate the space-time continuum of code",
  "intent_category": "development",
  "clarity_score": 6,
  "confidence": 0.7,
  "snarky_comment": "Back to work, because apparently we weren't done the first time",
  "resume_from_session": true
}
EOF

    log_info "Created fallback resume topic: $current_topic"
    return 0
}

log_debug "Resume feature loaded"

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Handler: SessionStart
# Orchestrates features for session initialization

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_HANDLER_SESSION_START_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_HANDLER_SESSION_START_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_LIB_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before session-start.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# handler_session_start - Handle SessionStart hook event
#
# Orchestrates session initialization:
# - Creates session directory
# - Initializes tracking counter (if enabled)
# - Launches cleanup in background (if enabled)
# - Generates resume topic (if enabled)
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, non-zero on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
handler_session_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "handler_session_start: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    if [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "handler_session_start: project_dir is required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "SessionStart handler: session=$session_id, project=$project_dir"

    # Get sidekick root for sourcing features
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Create session directory
    log_debug "Creating session directory"
    if ! util_create_session_dir "$session_id"; then
        log_error "Failed to create session directory"
        return 1
    fi

    # Source and initialize tracking feature
    if config_is_feature_enabled "tracking"; then
        log_debug "Sourcing tracking feature"
        # shellcheck source=src/sidekick/features/tracking.sh
        source "$sidekick_root/features/tracking.sh" || {
            log_error "Failed to source tracking feature"
            return 1
        }

        log_debug "Initializing tracking"
        if ! tracking_init "$session_id"; then
            log_warn "Failed to initialize tracking (non-fatal)"
        fi
    else
        log_debug "Tracking feature is disabled"
    fi

    # Source and launch cleanup feature
    if config_is_feature_enabled "cleanup"; then
        log_debug "Sourcing cleanup feature"
        # shellcheck source=src/sidekick/features/cleanup.sh
        source "$sidekick_root/features/cleanup.sh" || {
            log_error "Failed to source cleanup feature"
            return 1
        }

        log_debug "Launching cleanup process"
        if ! cleanup_launch "$session_id" "$project_dir"; then
            log_warn "Failed to launch cleanup (non-fatal)"
        fi
    else
        log_debug "Cleanup feature is disabled"
    fi

    # Source and execute resume feature
    if config_is_feature_enabled "resume"; then
        log_debug "Sourcing resume feature"
        # shellcheck source=src/sidekick/features/resume.sh
        source "$sidekick_root/features/resume.sh" || {
            log_error "Failed to source resume feature"
            return 1
        }

        log_debug "Generating resume topic"
        if ! resume_snarkify "$session_id" "$project_dir"; then
            log_warn "Failed to generate resume topic (non-fatal)"
        fi
    else
        log_debug "Resume feature is disabled"
    fi

    log_info "SessionStart handler completed successfully"
    return 0
}

log_debug "SessionStart handler loaded"

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# sidekick.sh - Main entry point for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Routes hook events to appropriate handlers and features.
#
# Commands:
#   sidekick.sh session-start      - SessionStart hook
#   sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit - UserPromptSubmit hook
#   sidekick.sh statusline         - Statusline rendering
#
# Hook commands receive JSON via stdin containing session_id, workspace, etc.
# Statusline command receives JSON via stdin containing session metadata.

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# BOOTSTRAP
# ============================================================================

# Get directory where this script lives
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

# Source shared library (loads all functions)
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/lib/common.sh"

# ============================================================================
# MAIN EXECUTION
# ============================================================================

main() {
    local command="${1:-}"

    # Validate command provided
    if [ -z "$command" ]; then
        usage
        exit 1
    fi

    # Load configuration cascade
    config_load

    # Route to appropriate handler based on command
    case "$command" in
        session-start)
            handle_session_start
            ;;
        user-prompt-submit)
            handle_user_prompt_submit
            ;;
        statusline)
            handle_statusline
            ;;
        --version|-v)
            show_version
            ;;
        --help|-h)
            usage
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown command: ${command}" >&2
            usage
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================
# HANDLERS
# ============================================================================

# Handle SessionStart hook
handle_session_start() {
    # Read JSON input from stdin
    local input
    input=$(cat)

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract session_id
    local session_id
    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")
    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Missing session_id in input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract project directory
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")

    # Initialize logging
    log_init "$session_id"
    log_info "SessionStart hook invoked for session ${session_id}"

    # Source and execute handler
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/handlers/session-start.sh"
    handler_session_start "$session_id" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

    log_info "SessionStart hook completed"
}

# Handle UserPromptSubmit hook
handle_user_prompt_submit() {
    # Read JSON input from stdin
    local input
    input=$(cat)

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract session_id

#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Handler: SessionStart
# Orchestrates features for session initialization

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_HANDLER_SESSION_START_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_HANDLER_SESSION_START_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_LIB_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before session-start.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# handler_session_start - Handle SessionStart hook event
#
# Orchestrates session initialization:
# - Creates session directory
# - Initializes tracking counter (if enabled)
# - Launches cleanup in background (if enabled)
# - Generates resume topic (if enabled)
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, non-zero on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
handler_session_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "handler_session_start: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    if [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "handler_session_start: project_dir is required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "SessionStart handler: session=$session_id, project=$project_dir"

    # Get sidekick root for sourcing features
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Create session directory
    log_debug "Creating session directory"
    if ! util_create_session_dir "$session_id"; then
        log_error "Failed to create session directory"
        return 1
    fi

    # Source and initialize tracking feature
    if config_is_feature_enabled "tracking"; then
        log_debug "Sourcing tracking feature"
        # shellcheck source=src/sidekick/features/tracking.sh
        source "$sidekick_root/features/tracking.sh" || {
            log_error "Failed to source tracking feature"
            return 1
        }

        log_debug "Initializing tracking"
        if ! tracking_init "$session_id"; then
            log_warn "Failed to initialize tracking (non-fatal)"
        fi
    else
        log_debug "Tracking feature is disabled"
    fi

    # Source and launch cleanup feature
    if config_is_feature_enabled "cleanup"; then
        log_debug "Sourcing cleanup feature"
        # shellcheck source=src/sidekick/features/cleanup.sh
        source "$sidekick_root/features/cleanup.sh" || {
            log_error "Failed to source cleanup feature"
            return 1
        }

        log_debug "Launching cleanup process"
        if ! cleanup_launch "$session_id" "$project_dir"; then
            log_warn "Failed to launch cleanup (non-fatal)"
        fi
    else
        log_debug "Cleanup feature is disabled"
    fi

    # Source and execute resume feature
    if config_is_feature_enabled "resume"; then
        log_debug "Sourcing resume feature"
        # shellcheck source=src/sidekick/features/resume.sh
        source "$sidekick_root/features/resume.sh" || {
            log_error "Failed to source resume feature"
            return 1
        }

        log_debug "Generating resume topic"
        if ! resume_snarkify "$session_id" "$project_dir"; then
            log_warn "Failed to generate resume topic (non-fatal)"
        fi
    else
        log_debug "Resume feature is disabled"
    fi

    log_info "SessionStart handler completed successfully"
    return 0
}

#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Tracking
# Request counting and static reminder injection

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_LIB_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before tracking.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_init - Initialize tracking counter for a session
#
# Creates the response_count file and initializes it to 0
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_init() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_init: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Initialize counter to 0
    echo "0" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking initialized: $counter_file"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_increment - Increment counter and return new value
#
# Atomically increments the counter and returns the new value
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the new counter value to stdout
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_increment() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_increment: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        log_warn "Counter file not found, initializing: $counter_file"
        tracking_init "$session_id" || return 1
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    # Increment
    local new_value=$((current + 1))

    # Write back
    echo "$new_value" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking incremented: $current -> $new_value"

    # Output new value
    echo "$new_value"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_get - Get current counter value
#
# Reads and returns the current counter value without incrementing
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the current counter value to stdout (0 if not initialized)
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_get() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_get: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$current"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_check_reminder - Check if static reminder is due
#
# Checks if we should output a static reminder based on the counter value
# and configured cadence. Loads static reminder text from files.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - count (current response count)
#   $2 - project_dir (optional, for project-specific reminders)
#
# Returns:
#   Prints reminder text to stdout if due, empty if not
#   Exit code: 0 always
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_check_reminder() {
    local count="$1"
    local project_dir="${2:-}"

    # Get cadence from config (default to 4)
    local cadence
    cadence=$(config_get "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE")
    [ -z "$cadence" ] && cadence=4

    # Check if reminder is due (count is multiple of cadence, but not 0)
    if [ "$count" -gt 0 ] && [ $((count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_debug "Static reminder due at count $count (cadence $cadence)"

        # Try to load reminder text from files (project overrides user)
        local reminder_text=""

        # Try project-specific reminder first
        if [ -n "$project_dir" ]; then
            local project_reminder="$project_dir/.claude/hooks/sidekick/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$project_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$project_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded project reminder from $project_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Fall back to user reminder if no project reminder
        if [ -z "$reminder_text" ]; then
            local sidekick_root
            sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
            local user_reminder="$sidekick_root/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$user_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$user_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded user reminder from $user_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Output reminder if we have one
        if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
            echo "$reminder_text"
        else
            log_debug "No static reminder file found"
        fi
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Tracking feature loaded"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Cleanup
# Session directory garbage collection

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_CLEANUP_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_CLEANUP_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_LIB_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before cleanup.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# cleanup_launch - Launch cleanup process in background
#
# Launches the cleanup_run function as a background process with PID tracking
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cleanup_launch() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "cleanup_launch: session_id and project_dir are required"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if cleanup is enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "cleanup"; then
        log_debug "Cleanup feature is disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    local cleanup_enabled
    cleanup_enabled=$(config_get "CLEANUP_ENABLED")
    if [ "$cleanup_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleanup is disabled (CLEANUP_ENABLED=$cleanup_enabled)"
        return 0
    fi

    log_info "Launching cleanup process in background"

    # Launch cleanup_run as background process
    # Export necessary vars for subprocess
    export SIDEKICK_ROOT
    export LOG_LEVEL
    SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Launch the cleanup process
    # We need to source this file in the subprocess so it has the cleanup_run function
    process_launch_background "$session_id" "cleanup" bash -c "
        # Source the common library and this feature in the subprocess
        source '$SIDEKICK_ROOT/lib/common.sh'
        source '$SIDEKICK_ROOT/features/cleanup.sh'

        # Re-load config
        config_load

        # Re-initialize logging for subprocess
        log_init '$session_id'

        # Run cleanup
        cleanup_run '$project_dir'
    "

    log_debug "Cleanup process launched with PID file"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# cleanup_run - Main cleanup logic
#
# Performs garbage collection on old session directories
# This function is called in the background process
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - project_dir (to locate sidekick tmp directory)
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cleanup_run() {
    local project_dir="$1"

    if [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "cleanup_run: project_dir is required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "Starting cleanup process"

    # Get configuration
    local min_count age_days dry_run
    min_count=$(config_get "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT")
    age_days=$(config_get "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS")
    dry_run=$(config_get "CLEANUP_DRY_RUN")

    # Defaults
    [ -z "$min_count" ] && min_count=5
    [ -z "$age_days" ] && age_days=2
    [ -z "$dry_run" ] && dry_run=false

    log_debug "Cleanup config: min_count=$min_count, age_days=$age_days, dry_run=$dry_run"

    # Determine tmp directory path
    # Could be user scope or project scope
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local tmp_dir="$sidekick_root/tmp"

    log_debug "Scanning tmp directory: $tmp_dir"

    # Verify tmp directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$tmp_dir" ]; then
        log_debug "No tmp directory found at: $tmp_dir"
        return 0
    fi

    # Safety check - must be under sidekick directory
    if [[ ! "$tmp_dir" =~ /.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp$ ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid tmp_dir path (safety check failed): $tmp_dir"
        return 1
    fi

    # Find all session directories matching UUID pattern
    # UUID format: 8-4-4-4-12 hex digits
    local uuid_pattern="[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}"
    local session_dirs=()

    while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do
        local basename
        basename=$(basename "$dir")
        # Validate UUID pattern for safety
        if [[ "$basename" =~ ^$uuid_pattern$ ]]; then
            session_dirs+=("$dir")
        else
            log_warn "Skipping non-UUID directory: $basename"
        fi
    done < <(find "$tmp_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -print0 2>/dev/null || true)

    local total_count=${#session_dirs[@]}
    log_debug "Found $total_count session directories"

    if [ $total_count -eq 0 ]; then
        log_debug "No session directories to process"
        return 0
    fi

    # Calculate age for each session directory
    # Age is based on the newest file in the directory
    local current_time
    current_time=$(date +%s)
    local age_threshold_seconds=$((age_days * 24 * 60 * 60))

    declare -A session_ages  # session_dir -> age_in_seconds
    local old_sessions=()

    for session_dir in "${session_dirs[@]}"; do
        # Find newest file in session directory
        # Using -printf '%T@' to get modification time as Unix timestamp
        local newest_file_time
        newest_file_time=$(find "$session_dir" -type f -printf '%T@\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -nr | head -1)

        if [ -z "$newest_file_time" ]; then
            # Empty directory or no files - use directory mtime
            newest_file_time=$(stat -c '%Y' "$session_dir" 2>/dev/null || echo "$current_time")
        fi

        # Convert to integer (truncate fractional seconds)
        newest_file_time=${newest_file_time%.*}

        # Calculate age in seconds
        local age=$((current_time - newest_file_time))
        session_ages["$session_dir"]=$age

        # Filter sessions older than threshold
        if [ $age -ge $age_threshold_seconds ]; then
            old_sessions+=("$session_dir")
            local age_days_actual=$((age / 86400))
            log_debug "Old session: $(basename "$session_dir") (${age_days_actual} days old)"
        fi
    done

    local old_count=${#old_sessions[@]}
    log_info "Found $old_count old sessions (>$age_days days) out of $total_count total"

    # Only cleanup if we exceed the minimum count threshold
    if [ $old_count -le $min_count ]; then
        log_info "Old session count ($old_count) <= threshold ($min_count), no cleanup needed"
        return 0
    fi

    # Sort old sessions by age (oldest first) for deletion
    # We'll remove sessions to bring the old count down to the threshold
    local sessions_to_remove=$((old_count - min_count))
    log_info "Will remove $sessions_to_remove oldest sessions to reach threshold of $min_count"

    # Create array of [age session_dir] for sorting
    local age_dir_pairs=()
    for session_dir in "${old_sessions[@]}"; do
        local age=${session_ages["$session_dir"]}
        age_dir_pairs+=("$age $session_dir")
    done

    # Sort by age (descending) and extract session directories
    IFS=$'\n' local sorted_pairs=($(sort -rn <<<"${age_dir_pairs[*]}"))
    unset IFS

    # Remove the oldest sessions
    local removed_count=0
    for i in $(seq 0 $((sessions_to_remove - 1))); do
        local pair="${sorted_pairs[$i]}"
        local session_dir="${pair#* }"  # Extract session_dir from "age session_dir"
        local age=${session_ages["$session_dir"]}
        local age_days_actual=$((age / 86400))
        local session_id
        session_id=$(basename "$session_dir")

        if [ "$dry_run" = "true" ]; then
            log_info "[DRY-RUN] Would remove: $session_id (${age_days_actual} days old)"
        else
            log_info "Removing: $session_id (${age_days_actual} days old)"
            if rm -rf "$session_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
                removed_count=$((removed_count + 1))
            else
                log_error "Failed to remove: $session_dir"
            fi
        fi
    done

    if [ "$dry_run" = "true" ]; then
        log_info "Dry-run complete: would have removed $sessions_to_remove sessions"
    else
        log_info "Cleanup complete: removed $removed_count of $sessions_to_remove sessions"
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Cleanup feature loaded"
